target_distribution_note: Adds 150 hard questions (L8-10) to address hard-question gap.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Enzyme Activity Test' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Based on the stimulus, which conclusion is most supported?

choices.A: The data prove causation in all contexts with no uncertainty.
choices.B: The relationship must be negative in all future trials.
choices.C: The results eliminate the need for controls.
choices.D: Results suggest an effect that should be tested with additional trials.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Based on the stimulus, which conclusion is most supported?

choices.A: The relationship must be negative in all future trials.
choices.B: The pattern suggests a positive relationship, though uncertainty limits precision.
choices.C: The results eliminate the need for controls.
choices.D: The data prove causation in all contexts with no uncertainty.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Enzyme Activity Test' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: The units used to record data
choices.B: The dependent variable itself
choices.C: The title of the experiment
choices.D: Differences in measurement technique between groups

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

questions.question: Based on the stimulus, which conclusion is most supported?

choices.A: The results eliminate the need for controls.
choices.B: The pattern suggests a positive relationship, though uncertainty limits precision.
choices.C: The relationship must be negative in all future trials.
choices.D: The data prove causation in all contexts with no uncertainty.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Enzyme Activity Test' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Based on the stimulus, which conclusion is most supported?

choices.A: The relationship must be negative in all future trials.
choices.B: The data are consistent with a trend rather than random variation alone.
choices.C: The results eliminate the need for controls.
choices.D: The data prove causation in all contexts with no uncertainty.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which change would most improve the experimental design?

choices.A: Change multiple variables at once to speed up results.
choices.B: Remove the control condition to simplify the study.
choices.C: Increase the number of trials and keep all variables constant except the independent variable.
choices.D: Record only the most extreme measurements.

questions.explanation: Reliability improves with replication and controlled variables.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: Differences in measurement technique between groups
choices.B: The units used to record data
choices.C: The title of the experiment
choices.D: The dependent variable itself

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: The title of the experiment
choices.B: The dependent variable itself
choices.C: Uncontrolled environmental conditions that vary across trials
choices.D: The units used to record data

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

questions.question: Based on the stimulus, which conclusion is most supported?

choices.A: The data prove causation in all contexts with no uncertainty.
choices.B: The results eliminate the need for controls.
choices.C: The pattern suggests a positive relationship, though uncertainty limits precision.
choices.D: The relationship must be negative in all future trials.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: Selection differences in samples assigned to groups
choices.B: The dependent variable itself
choices.C: The units used to record data
choices.D: The title of the experiment

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Water Quality Survey' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: If the observed trend continues, which prediction is most reasonable?

choices.A: The dependent variable would likely continue changing in the same direction, though the exact value is uncertain.
choices.B: The dependent variable will remain exactly constant.
choices.C: Future results will be identical in every trial.
choices.D: The dependent variable will reverse direction with certainty.

questions.explanation: Extrapolation uses the direction of the trend but avoids certainty about exact values.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: The units used to record data
choices.B: The dependent variable itself
choices.C: The title of the experiment
choices.D: A variable that changes with the independent variable and also affects the dependent variable

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

questions.question: If the observed trend continues, which prediction is most reasonable?

choices.A: Future results will be identical in every trial.
choices.B: The dependent variable will reverse direction with certainty.
choices.C: The dependent variable will remain exactly constant.
choices.D: The dependent variable would likely continue changing in the same direction, though the exact value is uncertain.

questions.explanation: Extrapolation uses the direction of the trend but avoids certainty about exact values.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Based on the stimulus, which conclusion is most supported?

choices.A: The relationship must be negative in all future trials.
choices.B: A change in the independent variable is associated with a measurable change in the dependent variable.
choices.C: The results eliminate the need for controls.
choices.D: The data prove causation in all contexts with no uncertainty.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Enzyme Activity Test' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: The units used to record data
choices.B: A variable that changes with the independent variable and also affects the dependent variable
choices.C: The dependent variable itself
choices.D: The title of the experiment

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: The title of the experiment
choices.B: The dependent variable itself
choices.C: The units used to record data
choices.D: Selection differences in samples assigned to groups

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

choices.A: The units used to record data

choices.C: The title of the experiment
choices.D: The dependent variable itself

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

questions.question: Which change would most improve the experimental design?

choices.A: Record only the most extreme measurements.
choices.B: Change multiple variables at once to speed up results.
choices.C: Increase the number of trials and keep all variables constant except the independent variable.
choices.D: Remove the control condition to simplify the study.

questions.explanation: Reliability improves with replication and controlled variables.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Water Quality Survey' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Based on the stimulus, which conclusion is most supported?

choices.A: The data prove causation in all contexts with no uncertainty.
choices.B: The results eliminate the need for controls.
choices.C: The relationship must be negative in all future trials.
choices.D: A change in the independent variable is associated with a measurable change in the dependent variable.

questions.explanation: Hard ACT Science items reward cautious inference: supported trend + attention to uncertainty/controls.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Planet Temperature Model' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: If the observed trend continues, which prediction is most reasonable?

choices.A: Future results will be identical in every trial.
choices.B: The dependent variable will remain exactly constant.
choices.C: The dependent variable would likely continue changing in the same direction, though the exact value is uncertain.
choices.D: The dependent variable will reverse direction with certainty.

questions.explanation: Extrapolation uses the direction of the trend but avoids certainty about exact values.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Water Quality Survey' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which change would most improve the experimental design?

choices.A: Remove the control condition to simplify the study.
choices.B: Record only the most extreme measurements.
choices.C: Change multiple variables at once to speed up results.
choices.D: Increase the number of trials and keep all variables constant except the independent variable.

questions.explanation: Reliability improves with replication and controlled variables.

questions.stimulus: A study titled 'Water Quality Survey' reports controlled trials and notes that measurement uncertainty may affect conclusions.
questions.question: Which factor is most likely a confounding variable in this study?

choices.A: The units used to record data
choices.B: The title of the experiment
choices.C: The dependent variable itself
choices.D: Uncontrolled environmental conditions that vary across trials

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.

choices.A: The title of the experiment
choices.B: The dependent variable itself
choices.C: Differences in measurement technique between groups
choices.D: The units used to record data

questions.explanation: Confounding variables are uncontrolled factors linked to both the independent and dependent variables.
